content.problemStatement: In this task, you are required to create a program that declares both an integer and a float for temperature readings.
constraints: No constraints are specified for the temperature values.
content.outputFormat: Two lines: first line the integer temperature reading, second line the float temperature reading.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The program declares an integer variable tempInt with value 25 and a float variable tempFloat with value 77.0, then prints them on separate lines.
Repeated example to emphasize the fixed output.

solution.approach: The task does not require any computation. Declare an integer variable with the value 25 and a float variable with the value 77.0, then print them on separate lines. No input handling is needed.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) – constant time because the program performs a fixed number of operations.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few primitive variables are stored.
